app.title: Hermes Task Board
col.desc.draft: Notes & ideas you haven't committed to yet.
col.desc.execute: Currently running — Hermes is on it.
col.desc.verify: Needs your review or a follow-up question.
col.desc.archive: Retired. Drag here to move out of sight.
toast.copy_failed: Copy failed — select the text manually.
field.title: Title
dep.hint: This task will stay in Plan until every dependency reaches at least the state selected. Verify means the depended-on task finished its attempts (waiting for your review); Done means you've accepted it.
placeholder.title: What needs doing?
placeholder.description: Optional. Markdown supported. Paste or drop images.
placeholder.tags: Type a tag, hit Enter — old ones autocomplete
empty.hint.draft: Click "New task" up top to jot down an idea.
empty.hint.plan: Drop a draft here or create one directly — queue it up for execution.
empty.hint.execute: Nothing is running. Planned tasks flow here automatically.
empty.hint.verify: No tasks waiting for review. Finished attempts land here for confirmation.
empty.hint.archive: Archive is empty. Verified tasks are filed here.
attempt.heading: Attempts
attempt.help: An Attempt is one execution of this task. You can think of it as one run — a task can be re-run any number of times, but usually once is enough. A new Attempt dispatches the task to Hermes afresh; re-running is only useful when you genuinely want to start from scratch. To continue an existing execution with more context, just send a message in the existing Attempt.
event.refresh_title: Reconnect to the Hermes session and pull the latest events
confirm.new_attempt.title: Start a new attempt?
confirm.new_attempt.body: This dispatches the task to Hermes as a fresh session. Only use this when the previous attempt needs to be redone from scratch — otherwise ask a follow-up question on the existing attempt.
editor.hint: Tip: paste, drop, or pick an image — it will be uploaded and a Markdown reference inserted.
editor.hint_no_upload: Image uploads are disabled: Hermes forwards the description verbatim to its LLM provider, which cannot fetch images served from this host. Configure Aliyun OSS in Settings → Integrations to enable image uploads.
editor.image_disabled_alert: Image upload is disabled. Configure an image host (Settings → Integrations → Aliyun OSS) so the image URL is reachable from Hermes's LLM provider.
settings.tags_helper: Tags group tasks and can carry an optional System Prompt that is prepended to Hermes whenever a task bearing that tag is dispatched. This lets you express cross-cutting instructions once — e.g. "when this task finishes, notify me on QQ" — and have every task carrying the tag automatically inherit it. A task's multiple tag prompts are concatenated in order.
settings.tags_empty: No tags yet. New tags are created automatically when you assign them to a task, and you can attach a System Prompt here.
tag.system_prompt_placeholder: e.g. When the task finishes, notify me on QQ with a short summary.
tag.system_prompt_hint: Optional. Leave blank to make the tag purely organisational. When set, this text is appended to the system prompt Hermes sees for every task carrying this tag.
confirm.delete_tag: Delete tag "{name}"? Tasks still referencing it will keep the name but lose the System Prompt.
schedule.heading: Schedules
schedule.help: Fire this task automatically on a schedule. Pick a mode (every N minutes/hours, daily, weekly, or monthly) and fill in the time — or switch to Advanced to write a raw cron expression. You can add as many schedules as you like; each runs independently. Scheduled fires create a new Attempt regardless of what column the task is currently in.
schedule.none: No schedules. Click below to fire this task automatically on the cadence you pick.
schedule.cron_help: Standard 5-field cron (min hour dom month dow), e.g. 0 9 * * 1-5 for weekdays at 9 AM.
schedule.err.time: Time must be in HH:MM format.
schedule.err.cron: Cron must be 5 whitespace-separated fields (min hour dom month dow).
settings.servers_helper: Each registered Hermes Server can hold multiple model profiles. Concurrency limits apply per server and per profile.
settings.models_helper: Each row is an agent profile on this Hermes server — the Hermes gateway calls them "model" in its API but conceptually they are agent profiles with distinct system prompts, tool sets, or personalities. Profile-level max_concurrent defaults to 5.
settings.archive_days: Auto-purge archive after N days (0 = never)
settings.oss_helper: Aliyun OSS is used as an image host for task descriptions. When disabled, images are stored locally under data/uploads/ and served by this server.
settings.oss_enable: Use Aliyun OSS for image uploads
settings.auth_intro_off: Password login is disabled. Enable it to require a username and password for everyone.
settings.auth_intro_on: Password login is enabled — signed in as {u}.
th.name: Name
login.title: Sign in
login.first_time_hint: To enable password login, open the app first and go to Settings → Access control.
